page: Amazon Web Services x8g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It features 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate top-tier memory performance, with cached read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 7395.63 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 456.97 GB/sec. Multi-core processing capabilities are strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25533.0 and a Passmark CPU physics score of 15316.76. Database and key-value operations perform exceptionally well, with Passmark database operations scoring 46392.67 and Redis SET operations achieving 132.1 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also highly efficient at 125.01 GB/sec.

The ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high resource density and power efficiency, making it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. Given the lack of local storage, storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by 64-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processors running at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB per core, with 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with cached read-only small block bandwidth at 7395.63 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 456.97 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25533.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 132.1 million operations per second. This server is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring high-density RAM and dedicated physical cores for demanding in-memory database and caching workloads.

1024.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Redis performance exceeding 132 million operations per second
